The CAP theorem, also known as the CAP principle, states that a distributed system can only simultaneously provide at most two of the following three properties:

1. Consistency: Every read receives the most recent write or an error (no stale reads).
2. Availability: Every request receives a (non-error) response, without guaranteeing that it contains the most recent write.
3. Partition Tolerance (or P): The system continues to operate despite arbitrary message loss or failure
